prose:
Frontier-orbital energies for formaldehyde (CH₂O) computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard'), via PySCF (chemkit frontier subcommand). Single-point calculation on the user-supplied geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). Density fitting (RI) was OFF — exact four-center integrals used. SCF converged in 8 cycles. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true). No warnings reported.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV (Koopmans-theorem orbital energy difference, not an excitation energy).
HOMO (index 7) = −7.6523067 eV
LUMO (index 8) = −1.7826355 eV
Total energy = −114.55325 Hartree (−3117.1528 eV)

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output JSON: formaldehyde_frontier_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json
